change_scope: Added re.sub(r',\s*([}\]])', r'\1', content) to strip LLM trailing commas before JSON parse; added debug logging for JSONDecodeError; added import re. Identical fix pattern to flash_triage.py.

findings.title: Regex operates on longer, free-text field values — minor increase over flash_triage
findings.detail: Red Team response JSON contains 'challenge', 'evidence', and 'suggested_fix' fields which contain free-form text that could theoretically contain literal ',}' or ',]'. Probability is very low — these are financial analysis narratives, not code or data structures. Same regex, same behavior as flash_triage.
findings.recommendation: Accept as-is. Monitor for any parsing failures in Red Team output.

verified_safe: import re — Python stdlib, already present at line 4
Regex ReDoS — O(n) linear, no catastrophic backtracking
Nested trailing commas — global re.sub covers all levels
4 automated test cases confirmed: trailing comma, valid JSON, free-text commas, markdown-wrapped — all PASS
notes: Same safe regex pattern as flash_triage.py. One LOW finding — free-text fields in Red Team output marginally increase the theoretical false-positive surface, but remains implausible for financial domain text.
